purpose: The commit fixes a queuing issue in HCI (Host Controller Interface) for Bluetooth, specifically when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set. This flag indicates that the HCI device is being unregistered, and the commit ensures that queuing commands fail in this scenario to prevent use-after-free (UAF) bugs.
The commit removes an unnecessary include statement in the n_hdlc.c file, specifically the asm/termios.h include. This change seems to be a code cleanup effort.
The commit updates documentation for the 'N/A' metric added for store operations in the perf c2c report table.
The commit fixes issues related to vma locking in hugetlb, addressing potential race conditions and ensuring proper synchronization for pmd sharing.
The commit adds documentation for the rt5682s codec, specifically for its AVDD and MICVDD supplies, which are used in the sc7180-trogador-kingoftown.dtsi file.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function by adding a missing iounmap() call. This ensures that resources are properly released when the device is removed.
The commit removes an unused work field from the struct mlx5e_async_ctx, which is a cleanup change with no functional impact.
The commit changes the type of a struct member 'key_idx' from unsigned to signed to avoid warnings from static checkers. This is done because the value -1 is assigned to it in certain cases.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfigs, with no functional change intended.
The commit introduces a new function, mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode(), to replace an open-coded check for CAN-FD mode in the mcp251xfd chip. This improves code readability and maintainability.

The commit modifies self-tests for Real-Time Clock (RTC) functionality. It introduces a check to skip tests when an RTC is not present, optimizing test runs on systems without this hardware component.
The commit removes a redundant assignment to the variable 'bytes' in the sound/pci/echoaudio/midi.c file, as it is never read and only serves to clean up a clang scan build warning.
The commit decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks, allowing rcuscale to be built without RCU Tasks. This change aims to reduce kernel size and complexity.
The commit removes duplicated text 'be split' in a header file, specifically in the context of KVM (Kernel-based Virtual Machine) and its memory management.
The commit reverts a change to the clk table population in drm/amd/display due to issues with dcfclk. It aims to prevent underflow until a better solution is implemented.
The commit adds a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU, enabling support for this specific hardware configuration.

The commit renames the 'pcie_port' struct to 'dw_pcie_rp' for consistency with other DW PCIe core driver entities. This change seems to be purely cosmetic and does not affect the functionality of the code.
The commit fixes a warning related to format specifiers in a printf-like function. It changes the format specifier to match the type of the argument being passed.
The commit fixes a branch range check issue in MIPS jump labels, ensuring correct signed comparisons to avoid negative offsets.
The commit removes a case from SetHwReg8188EU() and directly calls a function, as part of an effort to get rid of the unwanted hal layer.
The commit fixes sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static in the arm64 kernel's cpu_errata.c file.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from MDSS nodes, fixing a bogus binding issue.
The commit allocates space for arrays in struct ctrl_ref in preparation for allowing drivers to change the array size. It seems to be a preparatory step for future enhancements.
The commit adds support for new SoCs and PMICs in the Qualcomm socinfo driver.
The commit enhances the recognition of chip cuts in the rtl8xxxu driver, extending support beyond the previously known versions.
The commit removes a custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks, as the core already keeps a software enable count. This change seems to be a code optimization or cleanup.
The commit aims to reduce the usage of #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL by grouping related definitions together, improving code readability and maintainability.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to add functioning I2C nodes later, indicating a preparatory change for future functionality.
The commit fixes comments and typos in the thermal driver code, improving readability and accuracy.
The commit fixes an infinite loop issue in the igc driver, which occurs when the HW semaphore cannot be acquired during resource release. It adds error logging and quits the process.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology, specifically changing how bus clocks are defined and used.
The commit removes a pointless IS_SWAPFILE check, as it is always defined. This change simplifies the code by eliminating unnecessary conditions.
The commit implements GTP echo response, adding a feature to handle GTP messages, specifically echo requests. It ensures compliance with GTP specifications by responding to echo requests.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_* with cpumask_first_* where appropriate, improving the effectiveness of cpumask operations. It also introduces cpumask_first_zero() to handle a specific case.

The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR, which was failing and causing a message to be displayed on the console every time a roam occurred. The change ensures that the host driver no longer tries to get TIM information from firmware, fixing the issue.

The commit adds support for a platform without an amplifier, specifically for the alc5682 headset codec. It introduces a new board configuration, adl_rt5682, following Intel's BT offload design by connecting alc5682 to SSP0.
The commit fixes an issue with zerocopy sendmsg for protocols that don't support it, ensuring compatibility and preventing potential errors.
The commit improves the handling of xeCJK.sty in kerneldoc-preamble by testing its existence before loading, which resolves potential errors on certain distros like RHEL 9.
The commit allows certain macros to be used without reporting errors, specifically 'case' macros in checkpatch.pl. It seems to be a configuration change rather than a fix or new feature.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers for drm/simpledrm, improving code sharing and architecture.
The commit adds a jpeg decoding worker interface to ensure that three HWs run in parallel in MT8195. This is a new feature that enhances the functionality of the media system.
The commit removes pointless VPRINTK() calls and ata_scsi_dump_cdb() as they are redundant with existing tracepoints.
The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank in drm/amd/display. It allows the system to enter a low-power state when possible, optimizing power consumption.
The commit fixes a type mismatch between the expected and actual types of the reset_type parameter in the hqd_destroy() callback function, which was causing a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure.
The commit corrects the 'resets' property name in the pinctrl binding for stm32. It also adds a missing 'additionalProperties' check.

The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in the ath9k driver for GCC 12. It silences the warning by potentially changing how the code accesses or handles array indices, ensuring compatibility with stricter compiler checks.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get to simplify the code.
The commit reorganizes io_uring's fixed file installation and removal into the filetable code, making it more coherent and aligned with the rest of the io_uring implementation.
The commit updates device bindings to support separate nodes for G1 and G2 decoder blocks, enabling independent control via the power-domain controller.
The commit removes a macro (IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON) that was only used once inside another macro (IWL_DEVICE_BZ). It simplifies the code by directly incorporating the contents of the removed macro.
